rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN FOX VS. NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltff'S Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 28 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ADRIAN FOX, WILLIAM MCMULLEN, JR., SCOTT WINKLER Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Second Set Of Special Interrogatories, And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The tentative ruling is Class Action plaintiffs are entitled to discover the names and contact information of putative class members. Pioneer Electronics (USA), Inc. v. Super. Ct., 40 Cal.4th 360, 373 (2007). The court agrees with plaintiffs' interpretation of the application of Hill v. Nat'l Collegiate Athletic Assn. 7Cal. 4th 1 (1994) to the facts of this case. Balancing any privacy interest relating solely to contact information is weighed against a plaintiff's right to discovery. Discovery is permitted where appropriate safeguards are in place. Pioneer, 40 Cal. 4th at 371. The appropriate safeguard for this action appears to be through the use of a protective order, the scope of which will be one of the topics at the hearing. Monetary sanctions are denied based on the defendant's testing through this motion of its cited Vehicle, Financial and Civil Code sections as a basis for objection. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GELASIO ORTEGA VS. T.A.M.M.S. MOBILE SERVICES, INC., A CA. CORP.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend The Judgment To Add Non Party Alter Ego, Gordon Tamms As Judgment Debtor
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GELASIO ORTEGA's Motion To Amend The Judgment To Add Non Party Alter Ego, Gordon Tamms As Judgment Debtor. Continued to September 30, 2014 to allow Plaintiff to serve the moving papers on Defendant's counsel. Defendant's counsel states that he has not been provided with a copy of the moving papers and therefore Defendant has not had an opportunity to meaningfully respond. Plaintiff shall serve the moving papers on Defendant's counsel by September 8, 2014 or the motion will be denied. Defendant may file an opposition and Plaintiff may file a reply in accordance with CCP sec. 1005 contemplating the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RICHARD GREENLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Numbers 1.1, 2.6, 15.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 And 50.6, Request For Production Of Documents Set One, Numbers 1 And 2; And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2435
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 28 2014, Line 3, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHANNES HUWYLER Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Numbers 1.1, 2.6, 15.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 And 50.6, Request For Production Of Documents Set One, Numbers 1 And 2; And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2435. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties are directed to "meet and confer" prior to the hearing to attempt to resolve the issues raised by the motion to compel. The motion will be called last on the Discovery Motion docket. The served discovery responses did not include objections, so subsequent service of the verifications has no legal significance. Both parties' request for sanctions are denied if the parties resolve their issues prior to the hearing. If the issues are not resolved and the Court thereafter rules on the merits, sanctions against one or both parties will be considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 28 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF NANTONG JOYFUL FASHION CO., LTD, BEIJING MENGYAZI GARMENTS CO., LTD, BEIJING YANDI GARMENTS CO., LTD Motion To Compel Defendant To Provide Response To Plaintiff's Special Interoggatories Set One To Defendant George Georgiou. rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. JOHN CHIANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, Line 10, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHN CHIANG, THE OFFICE OF THE STATE CONTROLLER's Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Defendants (the "Controller") have not sufficiently established a likelihood of success on the merits or irreparable harm. Although the evidence establishes that the Controller may audit Plaintiff ("Thrivent") pursuant to CCP sec. 1571(a), the Controller does not provide sufficient evidence establishing that the current audit is limited to the inspection of "reasonably relevant" information. (Brovelli v. Sup. Ct. (1961) 56 Cal.2d 524, 529.) Further, the Controller has not established that it or the People of the State of California will be irreparably harmed if a preliminary injunction is not issued. The denial of the motion for preliminary injunction will not prevent the Controller from ever performing an audit of Thrivent. Rather, the audit will be delayed until Thrivent's Constitutional arguments are resolved at trial. The Controller has not made a sufficient showing of irreparable harm by delaying the determination of these issues until after a trial on the merits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EDNA CAMPBELL VS. PACIFIC MARITIME ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 28, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT PACIFIC MARITIME ASSOCIATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The NLRA preempts plaintiff's claims. The allegations of the complaint demonstrate that plaintiff's action is based on her concerted union activities. See Par. 14 of the complaint. The court rejects plaintiff's mixed-motive argument. See Henry v. Intercontinental Radio, Inc. (1984) 155 Cal.App.3d 707, 715. Merely labeling certain claims as anti-discrimination causes of action does not circumvent the NLRA preemption. See Luke v. Collotype Labels USA, Inc. (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 1463, 1472-1473 and Pitts v. Plumbers & Steamfitters Local Union No. 33 (S.D. Iowa 2010) 718 F.Supp.2d 1010. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
